title: Livestock Disease Surveillance And Pastoralist Livelihood Risk In East Africa
summary: Build a verified workflow for detecting livestock disease outbreaks and estimating pastoralist livelihood risk in East Africa, linking syndromic surveillance data, satellite-derived forage conditions, and market-price anomalies.
why_it_matters: East Africa's pastoralists manage over 60 percent of the region's livestock, providing food and income for 20 million people. Diseases like Rift Valley fever, contagious bovine pleuropneumonia, and peste des petits ruminants can wipe out herds in weeks. Satellite-based forage monitoring exists but linking vegetation conditions to disease risk and market responses at actionable lead times is underdeveloped.

success_metrics.target: Every disease-risk signal links surveillance, forage, and market data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
